title: Design System: Jake Mosher — Client Landing

display.purpose: Hero headline only; two stacked clauses.

headline.purpose: Section titles; color keyed to the band.

title.purpose: Sub-section heads: offer items, process steps, project names.

label.purpose: Availability chip and small inline labels; sentence case.

shadows.name: framed-media
shadows.purpose: Lifts project screenshots and the About photo off the page.

shadows.name: button-rest
shadows.purpose: Resting lift under the primary CTA.

shadows.name: dark-band-glow
shadows.value: radial-gradient(60% 55% at 82% 8%, color-mix(in oklab, #14b8a6 26%, transparent), transparent 60%)
shadows.purpose: Atmospheric teal glow on the dark proof band (not a box shadow).

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Default easing for reveals and lifts.

motion.name: reveal
motion.value: opacity + translateY(16-30px), animation-timeline: view(), range entry 8% cover 34%
motion.purpose: Section scroll-reveal; visible by default, gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.

motion.name: facet-drift
motion.purpose: Foreground facet plane; leads the page.

motion.name: stone-drift
motion.purpose: Background stone plane; lags slower than the page.

breakpoints.name: nav-compact
breakpoints.purpose: Nav drops the Work link, keeps the CTA.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one CTA. Resting rust-soft with ink text; hover deepens to rust with white text and a small lift.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: #ce6c36; color: #1b201d; font-family: Switzer, system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.05rem; padding: 0.9rem 1.6rem; border-radius: 12px; text-decoration: none; box-shadow: 0 8px 22px -14px rgba(194,98,46,0.7); transition: background-color 220ms ease, color 220ms ease, transform 160ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1), box-shadow 220ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #a64f22; color: #fff; transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 16px 30px -14px rgba(166,79,34,0.75); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #a64f22; outline-offset: 3px; }

components.name: Ghost Link
components.description: Secondary action: pine text with a teal-tinted underline that brightens on hover.
components.css: .ds-link-ghost { color: #0f5e54; font-weight: 600; font-size: 1.05rem; text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 2px solid rgba(20,184,166,0.4); padding-bottom: 2px; transition: color 160ms ease, border-color 160ms ease; } .ds-link-ghost:hover { color: #0f8c7e; border-bottom-color: #14b8a6; }

components.name: Nav CTA
components.description: Hairline pine pill in the top nav.
components.css: .ds-nav-cta { color: #0f5e54; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; border: 1px solid rgba(15,94,84,0.35); padding: 0.5rem 1rem; border-radius: 999px; transition: background-color 160ms ease, border-color 160ms ease; } .ds-nav-cta:hover { background: rgba(20,184,166,0.12); border-color: rgba(15,94,84,0.55); }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Contact field: paper-raised fill, hairline border, teal focus glow.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.4rem; } .ds-field span { font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.95rem; color: #1b201d; } .ds-field input { font-family: Switzer, system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; color: #1b201d; background: #fff; border: 1px solid #d6d2ca; border-radius: 10px; padding: 0.75rem 0.9rem; transition: border-color 140ms ease, box-shadow 140ms ease; } .ds-field input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #0f8c7e; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(20,184,166,0.22); }

components.name: Faceted-Stone Banner
components.description: Signature between-section motif: small textured triangles scattered asymmetrically over a pale stone field, a few bleeding past the edges. On scroll the facets drift faster than the page and the stone drifts slower (three planes). Static under reduced motion.
components.css: .ds-banner { position: relative; width: 100%; height: clamp(240px, 32vh, 360px); background: #efece7; border-block: 1px solid #e4e1db; overflow: visible; } .ds-banner-stone { position: absolute; inset: 0; background-image: url('/src/assets/textures/stone-1.jpg'); background-size: 480px; mix-blend-mode: multiply; opacity: 0.45; } .ds-banner-facets { position: absolute; inset: 0; } .ds-f { position: absolute; aspect-ratio: 1; } .ds-f-up { clip-path: polygon(50% 0, 100% 100%, 0 100%); } .ds-f-down { clip-path: polygon(0 0, 100% 0, 50% 100%); } .ds-f::after { content: ''; position: absolute; inset: 0; background-image: url('/src/assets/textures/stone-2.jpg'); background-size: cover; mix-blend-mode: multiply; opacity: 0.14; }

narrative.overview: A jewel of teal, cut and set into a slab of warm, textured stone. The surface is quiet near-white paper with a whisper of real grain; the color is committed vibrant teal carrying the page while a single burnt rust does the asking. Geometry is the signature: small triangular facets filled with genuine stone texture, scattered asymmetrically and breaking their frame. Friendly, vibrant, and confident for a non-technical client who wants a great site without the headache. Warmth comes from the rust accent and stone texture, never a warm-cream body background. Depth is built from tonal bands and a stone field that drifts slower than the page, not from drop shadows.
keyCharacteristics: Committed jewel-teal lead; rust as a rare, warm accent.
Crisp near-white paper base (never warm cream), warmed by texture and accent.
Faceted-stone geometry as the signature motif, asymmetric and frame-breaking.
Tactile but calm: soft lifts, real texture, generous type, nothing noisy.
Bricolage Grotesque + Switzer; no Inter, no Fraunces, no mono.

rules.name: The Teal-Leads Rule
rules.body: Teal carries the surface; rust is a minority accent on roughly the CTA and small highlights only. They never co-lead.

rules.name: The Pine-for-Text Rule
rules.body: Bright teal and teal-deep are fills, never small text (both fail AA on light). Any teal-colored text uses Pine (#0f5e54). On the dark band, bright teal is fine.

rules.name: The Texture-Carries-Warmth Rule
rules.body: Warmth lives in the rust accent and the stone grain, never in the body background. The base stays a crisp off-white.

rules.name: The Two-Tone Headline Rule
rules.body: The hero splits across the you/me divide: 'You bring the idea.' in ink, 'I bring the craft.' in pine.

rules.name: The Band-Keyed Heading Rule
rules.body: Section-heading color signals the band: ink on paper, pine on tinted surface, cream on the dark band.

rules.name: The No-Default-Fonts Rule
rules.body: Never reach for Inter, Fraunces, or a mono 'for technical flavor.' This is a client surface, not a dev tool.

rules.name: The Tonal-Band Rule
rules.body: Depth comes from the paper/surface/dark band rhythm and the slower-drifting stone, not from drop shadows on cards.

dos: Do let teal lead and keep rust a minority accent (CTA + small highlights only).
Do use pine for any teal-colored text; bright teal and teal-deep are fills.
Do carry warmth through the rust accent and stone texture, on a crisp off-white base.
Do key section-heading color to the band (ink on paper, pine on surface, cream on dark).
Do keep copy warm and contracted, client-language, no jargon.
Do gate every entrance/parallax behind prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference and keep content visible by default.
Do let facets break their frame; the bleed is intentional.
donts: Don't use a warm cream / sand / beige body background; the base is crisp near-white.
Don't ship the generic-AI look: no gradient text, glassmorphism, hero-metric template, identical icon-card grids, or eyebrow-on-every-section.
Don't drift cold-corporate (navy-and-gradient SaaS) or loud-flashy (over-animated agency, counter-rotating banners).
Don't go synthwave / neon-grid, and don't do the mint-green 'fresh startup' template.
Don't add visual noise; the facets are sparse and asymmetric on purpose.
Don't reach for Inter, Fraunces, or mono.
Don't put bright teal or teal-deep on small text (both fail WCAG AA on light).
Don't separate elements with drop-shadowed cards; use a tonal band or hairline.
